title: SSRF Defense, Prototype-Safe Data, Terminal Injection, Paths, and Resource Limits · Type-System Trace: Pokedex Ts Path Data Policy
summary: Redirects escape the fixed host, parsed objects are merged into state, remote text contains ANSI or bidi controls, save paths are arbitrary, and response or command sizes are unbounded. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts path data policy increment in deny-by-default TypeScript trust-boundary policy. Learners progress through…
objectives: Use an application-owned data location, reject traversal and unexpected file types, minimize permissions, and document symlink limits.
Normalize or visibly escape control, ANSI, bidi, and non-printing sequences from commands, API payloads, and files.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts Path Data Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 7771 during an offline mobile-library catalog migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce redirect, prototype, terminal, path, privacy, and resource boundaries; unknown external values must not cross the runtime validator; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
